data.text: I’ll use the HELIX design workflow because this bead is explicitly about producing a solution design document and wiring it into the existing HELIX artifacts. I’m going to read the governing feature, nearby design docs, and any TP-019 stub before editing.

The worktree already has unrelated `.agents/skills/*` changes; I’ll leave them untouched and stage only the HELIX docs I edit. I’ve confirmed `SD-019` is already occupied, so I’m looking at the local document style and the existing agent evaluation command surface before creating `SD-023`.

The implementation surface is partly present in `compare_adapter.go`, `grade.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`, with some FEAT-019 commands routed through service helpers already. I’m checking the exact record shapes so the design document doesn’t describe a parallel architecture that conflicts with the code.

I’ve got enough context to write the design now. The important decision is that replay should not invent a new evidence store: execution bundles own immutable attempt artifacts and base/result refs, while session logs own harness/model/token/cost and, where available, the prompt transcript; replay reads both through a deterministic precedence chain.

The new SD is drafted with the requested architecture decisions. I’m adding explicit upstream links now so FEAT-019 and TP-019 both point to the design authority rather than leaving TP-019 as an orphaned test matrix.
The links are in place. I’m doing a focused verification pass now: checking the new artifact is discoverable by ID, the links resolve, and the acceptance points have explicit sections in SD-023.

There isn’t a repo-local `./ddx` binary, but the installed `ddx` has a document graph validator. I’m running that plus markdownlint on the three touched files; if the global linter is stricter than this repo’s conventions, I’ll report that rather than churn unrelated style.

`ddx doc validate` found the expected new SD, but my first link added a graph cycle by putting `SD-023` in FEAT-019’s `depends_on` while SD-023 depends on FEAT-019. I’m removing that frontmatter dependency and keeping the human-facing link; then I’ll clean the new SD’s markdown style separately from pre-existing FEAT/TP lint noise.

The document graph now recognizes `SD-023` without the new FEAT/SD cycle; `ddx doc validate` still reports two pre-existing graph warnings outside this bead. The new SD itself passes markdownlint, so I’m staging only the three intended docs and running a final diff check before committing.

Done. Committed:

`8608519a docs(design): add agent evaluation solution design [ddx-3b91ca7a]`

What changed:
- Added [SD-023-agent-evaluation.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3b91ca7a-20260421T122511-cd20d653/docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-023-agent-evaluation.md:1), covering comparison arm isolation, grading pipeline, replay source-of-truth, benchmark, quorum, compare, grade, and replay relationships.
- Linked it from [FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3b91ca7a-20260421T122511-cd20d653/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md:88).
- Linked it from [TP-019-agent-evaluation.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3b91ca7a-20260421T122511-cd20d653/docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-019-agent-evaluation.md:11).

Verification:
- `markdownlint docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-023-agent-evaluation.md` passed.
- `ddx doc show SD-023` resolves the new artifact and shows `TP-019` as a dependent.
- `ddx doc validate` exits 0, with two pre-existing warnings unrelated to this bead.
- Pre-existing dirty `.agents/skills/*` changes were left untouched.
